dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mapped channel breakout to Donchian Channel (dc_upper, dc_lower, dc_middle) with lookback_period_n=8 implicitly set. Entry on breakout above/below channel boundaries. Exit on closure back inside channel (crossing dc_middle) or breach of opposite boundary. Direction is bidirectional (LONG on upside breakout, SHORT on downside breakout).

backtest_rule.reason: Mapped channel breakout to Donchian Channel (dc_upper, dc_lower, dc_middle) with lookback_period_n=8 implicitly set. Entry on breakout above/below channel boundaries. Exit on closure back inside channel (crossing dc_middle) or breach of opposite boundary. Direction is bidirectional (LONG on upside breakout, SHORT on downside breakout).

source_rule_text: A trader should adjust the lookback period of a channel breakout indicator based on the trend magnitude they wish to capture—larger lookback periods detect larger trends with fewer false signals, while smaller periods detect smaller trends with higher sensitivity.
